perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this letter agreement, (i) "Representative" shall mean, as to any person, its directors, officers, employees, agents and advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, attorneys and accountants) and debt and equity financing sources and their advisors and Representatives (but shall not include any debt and equity financing sources that enter into a confidentiality agreement reasonably <PAGE> 2 acceptable to the Disclosing Party, which either names Disclosing Party as a third party beneficiary or to which Disclosing Party is made a party, and an executed copy of which is provided to Disclosing Party); and (ii) "person" shall be broadly interpreted to include, without limitation, any corporation, company, partnership, other entity or individual.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this letter agreement, (i) "Representative" shall mean, as to any person, its directors, officers, and employees only; and (ii) "person" shall be broadly interpreted to include, without limitation, any corporation, company, partnership, other entity or individual. Advisors, agents, and debt/equity financing sources are specifically excluded from the definition of Representative.
perturbation.explanation: This change narrows the definition of 'Representative' by excluding advisors, agents, and debt/equity financing sources. This contradicts later sections that hold the Receiving Party responsible for breaches by its 'Representatives', potentially exempting actions by those now-excluded parties and creating ambiguity about liability. This contradicts with 'The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this letter agreement by it and secondarily responsible for any breach of the terms of this letter agreement by its Representatives.' which later include those representatives.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this letter agreement by it and secondarily responsible for any breach of the terms of this letter agreement by its Representatives. The parties agree that notwithstanding the generality of the foregoing, the existence of any discussions shall not be disclosed in any court, governmental or other similar proceeding except as expressly permitted herein. Notwithstanding the definition of Evaluation Material, nothing contained herein shall be deemed to prohibit the Disclosing Party or the Receiving Party from utilizing any information obtained pursuant to discovery or other mediation, arbitration, court, or administrative proceedings even though such information also was provided hereunder as Evaluation Material; provided that neither the Disclosing Party or the Receiving Party shall utilize any Evaluation Material in connection with such proceeding unless acquired in such proceeding.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Representative' by excluding advisors, agents, and debt/equity financing sources, which directly contradicts the contradicted text that holds the Receiving Party secondarily responsible for breaches by its 'Representatives,' including those that are now excluded. This creates a clear in-text contradiction because actions by advisors, agents, and debt/equity financing sources would no longer be covered under the 'Representative' definition, potentially exempting the Receiving Party from liability for their breaches, whereas the contradicted text implies that those breaches would result in liability. The original text did include these parties in the definition of representatives.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives are requested pursuant to, or required by, applicable law or regulation (including, without limitation, any rule, regulation or policy statement of any national securities exchange, market or automated quotation system on which any of the Receiving Party's securities are listed or quoted) or by legal process to disclose any Evaluation Material or any other information concerning the Disclosing Party or the Proposed Transaction, the Receiving Party shall provide the Disclosing Party with prompt notice of such request or requirement in order to enable the Disclosing Party (i) to seek an appropriate protective order or other remedy, (ii) to consult with the Receiving Party with respect to the Receiving Party's taking steps to resist or narrow the scope of such request or legal process, or (iii) to waive compliance, in whole or in part, with the terms of this letter agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the Receiving Party is requested pursuant to, or required by, applicable law or regulation (including, without limitation, any rule, regulation or policy statement of any national securities exchange, market or automated quotation system on which any of the Receiving Party's securities are listed or quoted) or by legal process to disclose any Evaluation Material or any other information concerning the Disclosing Party or the Proposed Transaction, the Receiving Party shall provide the Disclosing Party with prompt notice of such request or requirement in order to enable the Disclosing Party (i) to seek an appropriate protective order or other remedy, (ii) to consult with the Receiving Party with respect to the Receiving Party's taking steps to resist or narrow the scope of such request or legal process, or (iii) to waive compliance, in whole or in part, with the terms of this letter agreement. The Representatives of the Receiving Party are explicitly exempt from this notification requirement.
perturbation.explanation: This change states that only the Receiving Party is required to provide notice of requests for disclosure, explicitly exempting its Representatives. This introduces a contradiction because other clauses still hold the Receiving Party responsible for the actions of its Representatives, while this section limits their obligations regarding legal disclosures. This creates uncertainty about whether the Receiving Party is ultimately liable if a Representative fails to notify and discloses confidential information and conflicts with 'The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this letter agreement by it and secondarily responsible for any breach of the terms of this letter agreement by its Representatives.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this letter agreement by it and secondarily responsible for any breach of the terms of this letter agreement by its Representatives. The parties agree that notwithstanding the generality of the foregoing, the existence of any discussions shall not be disclosed in any court, governmental or other similar proceeding except as expressly permitted herein. Notwithstanding the definition of Evaluation Material, nothing contained herein shall be deemed to prohibit the Disclosing Party or the Receiving Party from utilizing any information obtained pursuant to discovery or other mediation, arbitration, court, or administrative proceedings even though such information also was provided hereunder as Evaluation Material; provided that neither the Disclosing Party or the Receiving Party shall utilize any Evaluation Material in connection with such proceeding unless acquired in such proceeding.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly exempts Representatives from the notification requirement, while the contradicted text states the Receiving Party is 'secondarily responsible for any breach of the terms of this letter agreement by its Representatives.' This creates a direct conflict because the Receiving Party's responsibility for its Representatives' actions is undermined by the exemption, introducing uncertainty and a potential contradiction regarding liability for non-disclosure.

perturbation.original_text: For a period (the "Restricted Period") commencing with the date of this letter agreement and ending on the earlier of (i) 15 months after the termination of discussions between the parties with respect to a Proposed Transaction and (ii) the occurrence of a "Significant Event" (as defined below), neither party hereto nor any of its Representatives shall, without the prior written consent of the other party or its board of directors or any committee thereof delegated the responsibility for such matters: (a) acquire, offer to acquire, or agree to acquire, directly or indirectly, by purchase or otherwise, any voting securities or direct or indirect rights to acquire any voting securities of the other party or any subsidiary of the other party, or of any successor to or person in control of the other party, or any material assets of the other party or any subsidiary or division of the other party or of any such successor or controlling person; (b) make, or in any way participate, directly or indirectly, in any "solicitation" of "proxies" to vote (as such terms are used in the rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC")), or seek to advise or influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of any voting securities of the other party; (c) make any public announcement with respect to, or submit a proposal or offer (with or without conditions) in connection with any of the foregoing; (d) form, join or in any way participate in a "group" as defined in Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the "Exchange Act"), in connection with any of the foregoing; (e) otherwise act or seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of the other party; (f) take any action that could reasonably be expected to require the other party to make a public announcement regarding the possibility of any of the events described in clauses (a) through (e) above; or
perturbation.changed_text: For a period (the "Restricted Period") commencing with the date of this letter agreement and ending on the earlier of (i) 15 months after the termination of discussions between the parties with respect to a Proposed Transaction and (ii) the occurrence of a "Significant Event" (as defined below), neither party hereto shall, without the prior written consent of the other party or its board of directors or any committee thereof delegated the responsibility for such matters: (a) acquire, offer to acquire, or agree to acquire, directly or indirectly, by purchase or otherwise, any voting securities or direct or indirect rights to acquire any voting securities of the other party or any subsidiary of the other party, or of any successor to or person in control of the other party, or any material assets of the other party or any subsidiary or division of the other party or of any such successor or controlling person; (b) make, or in any way participate, directly or indirectly, in any "solicitation" of "proxies" to vote (as such terms are used in the rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC")), or seek to advise or influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of any voting securities of the other party; (c) make any public announcement with respect to, or submit a proposal or offer (with or without conditions) in connection with any of the foregoing; (d) form, join or in any way participate in a "group" as defined in Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the "Exchange Act"), in connection with any of the foregoing; (e) otherwise act or seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of the other party; (f) take any action that could reasonably be expected to require the other party to make a public announcement regarding the possibility of any of the events described in clauses (a) through (e) above; or
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the restriction on the parties' 'Representatives' during the Restricted Period regarding acquiring voting securities, soliciting proxies, etc. This contradicts the clause holding the parties responsible for their Representatives' actions, as the Representatives are now free to engage in activities that the parties themselves are prohibited from doing. This contradictes to the secondarily responsibility of any breach of the terms of this letter agreement by its Representatives.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this letter agreement by it and secondarily responsible for any breach of the terms of this letter agreement by its Representatives. The parties agree that notwithstanding the generality of the foregoing, the existence of any discussions shall not be disclosed in any court, governmental or other similar proceeding except as expressly permitted herein. Notwithstanding the definition of Evaluation Material, nothing contained herein shall be deemed to prohibit the Disclosing Party or the Receiving Party from utilizing any information obtained pursuant to discovery or other mediation, arbitration, court, or administrative proceedings even though such information also was provided hereunder as Evaluation Material; provided that neither the Disclosing Party or the Receiving Party shall utilize any Evaluation Material in connection with such proceeding unless acquired in such proceeding.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts both the parties and their representatives from certain actions during the Restricted Period. The changed text removes the restriction on the Representatives, but the 'Contradicted Text' holds the parties 'secondarily responsible for any breach of the terms of this letter agreement by its Representatives.' This creates a contradiction, because the Representatives can now engage in activities that the parties are forbidden from doing but for which the parties are still liable, making the enforcement of the secondary responsibility unclear and inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: In that case, or in the event that the Disclosing Party, in its sole discretion, so requests or the Proposed Transaction is not consummated by the Receiving Party, the Receiving party shall, upon the Disclosing Party's written request, promptly deliver to the Disclosing Party all Evaluation Material, and, at the Receiving Party's election, return or destroy (provided that any such destruction shall be certified by a duly authorized Representative of the Receiving Party) all copies, reproductions, summaries, analyses or extracts thereof, including any electronic or computer file copies, or based thereon in the Receiving Party's possession or in the possession of any Representative of the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: In that case, or in the event that the Disclosing Party, in its sole discretion, so requests or the Proposed Transaction is not consummated by the Receiving Party, the Receiving party shall, upon the Disclosing Party's written request, promptly deliver to the Disclosing Party all Evaluation Material, and, at the Receiving Party's election, return or destroy all copies, reproductions, summaries, analyses or extracts thereof, including any electronic or computer file copies, or based thereon in the Receiving Party's possession. The Receiving Party is NOT responsible for destruction certification from its Representative.
perturbation.explanation: This change specifies that destruction does not have to be certified by the Representative. Which contradictes with the fact that 'Representative's actions make the party responsible.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this letter agreement by it and secondarily responsible for any breach of the terms of this letter agreement by its Representatives. The parties agree that notwithstanding the generality of the foregoing, the existence of any discussions shall not be disclosed in any court, governmental or other similar proceeding except as expressly permitted herein. Notwithstanding the definition of Evaluation Material, nothing contained herein shall be deemed to prohibit the Disclosing Party or the Receiving Party from utilizing any information obtained pursuant to discovery or other mediation, arbitration, court, or administrative proceedings even though such information also was provided hereunder as Evaluation Material; provided that neither the Disclosing Party or the Receiving Party shall utilize any Evaluation Material in connection with such proceeding unless acquired in such proceeding.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that destruction of evaluation material shall be certified by a duly authorized Representative of the Receiving Party. The changed text removes the requirement for certification from the representative. The contradicted text states that the Receiving Party is secondarily responsible for any breach of the agreement by its Representatives. By removing the certification requirement, the Receiving Party is no longer responsible for the Representative's actions regarding destruction certification, which directly contradicts the clause establishing secondary responsibility for the Representative's actions.
